title: Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPZ) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPZ) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 89/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 8% growth rate is supported by Domino's highly efficient franchised model. Strong unit economics allow for continued global expansion, while tech integrations (like Uber Eats) and loyalty program optimizations drive incremental transaction growth, funneling directly into high-margin royalty streams.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 7.5% discount rate reflects the immense durability of the franchised cash flows and their dominant market share, balanced slightly by macroeconomic pressures on lower-income consumers.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate is appropriate for a mature but dominant QSR brand that can consistently pass on inflation through pricing and continues to capture market share from independent pizzerias globally.

thesisTitle: The Technology Company that Sells Pizza
thesisParagraphs: Domino's Pizza is fundamentally misunderstood if viewed merely as a restaurant. It is an e-commerce and logistics powerhouse that happens to deliver pizza. Their proprietary point-of-sale systems, world-class mobile app, and massive loyalty program give them a data advantage that no competitor can match. This technological edge translates directly into unparalleled operational efficiency and phenomenal returns on invested capital.

The transition to a nearly 100% franchised model makes the corporate entity incredibly asset-light. The cash generated by the business is massive and highly predictable. Even in challenging macroeconomic environments, pizza remains a resilient "value" meal. With the recent strategic shift to partner with third-party aggregators while maintaining control of their own drivers, Domino's is perfectly positioned to capture even more transaction volume, making the current valuation highly attractive.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Finviz (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is Domino's considered undervalued here?
faq.answer: The DCF model heavily weights their massive, high-margin free cash flow generation. The market sometimes penalizes them for short-term sales fluctuations, but their asset-light franchise model and international expansion runway suggest the long-term cash flows are worth more than the current price.

faq.question: Does the Uber Eats partnership help or hurt?
faq.answer: The model assumes it helps significantly. By capturing demand from the Uber Eats platform while still using their own drivers, Domino's gains incremental high-income customers without sacrificing logistical efficiency or data control.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice.

prospectVerdictDetail: Domino's Pizza possesses a surprisingly robust economic moat within the QSR space, driven heavily by its technological superiority, efficient franchise model, and massive scale advantage in delivery logistics. The company generates phenomenal returns on invested capital and consistent free cash flow. While the post-pandemic delivery normalization presented headwinds, new partnerships (like Uber Eats) and continuous menu innovation demonstrate strong management adaptability. The heavily franchised model ensures asset-light, high-margin royalty streams.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Domino's maintains strong momentum through technological advantages and strategic partnerships that drive order volume and defend market share against third-party delivery aggregators.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs Peers
factors.rationale: Domino's consistently outpaces broader QSR growth through reliable global store expansion and positive same-store sales driven by order frequency.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: It remains the undisputed leader in pizza delivery. Strategic partnerships, such as listing on Uber Eats while using their own drivers, allow them to capture aggregator demand without losing logistical control.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: While highly promotional, Domino's has demonstrated the ability to pass on food and labor cost inflation through subtle menu price increases and adjustments to their Mix & Match deals.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Continuous, targeted menu innovation (like new crusts, loaded tots, and specialized pizzas) keeps the brand relevant and drives ticket size without overly complicating operations.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: System sales growing ~7-8% globally (same-store + net new units). US comps of 3-5% are outperforming Pizza Hut and Papa John's. International same-store sales have recovered from the post-COVID pullback. DPZ is the fastest-growing major pizza chain globally and competitive with the best QSR performers (CMG, MCD).

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The moat is wide, anchored by an exceptional loyalty program, proprietary technology stack, and an incredibly efficient, vertically integrated supply chain.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs are generally low in fast food, but Domino's massive loyalty program (Domino's Rewards) creates significant behavioral lock-in and repeat purchasing habits.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Scale density creates a powerful localized network effect. More stores mean shorter delivery times, hotter food, lower delivery costs per order, and improved unit economics.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The brand IP is incredibly strong globally. Regulatory risks are minimal, though localized labor laws affecting gig workers or minimum wage increases require constant navigation.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The nearly 100% franchised model is incredibly asset-light for the corporate entity, resulting in massive free cash flow generation and spectacular returns on invested capital.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is highly positive, driven by strong execution of their revamped loyalty program and the incremental transaction growth from third-party aggregator partnerships.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts frequently revise estimates upward as Domino's proves its ability to drive transaction growth and maintain margins despite a challenging macroeconomic environment for lower-income consumers.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is focused on their successful tech integrations, massive international expansion potential, and resilience as a 'value' option during inflationary periods.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management execution is elite. Capital allocation heavily favors shareholders through consistent, substantial dividend increases and aggressive share repurchase programs funded by robust free cash flow.

prospectKeyRisks: Severe and prolonged commodity inflation (cheese, wheat) pressuring franchisee margins and slowing new store development.
Intense competition from well-funded third-party delivery platforms fundamentally changing consumer ordering habits away from direct-to-brand channels.
A tight labor market leading to sustained wage inflation and difficulty staffing delivery driver positions.
prospectKeyCatalysts: International unit growth accelerating toward 1,500+ net new stores annually as India, China, and emerging market penetration deepens — this could drive system sales growth to 8-10% and justify a premium multiple
Aggregator partnership expansion to additional platforms (DoorDash) in the US would unlock another wave of incremental orders and prove the multi-platform delivery strategy
Menu innovation driving average ticket growth — new premium products and occasion expansion (breakfast, late-night) could lift per-store economics and support franchisee unit-level returns
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored DPZ at 82/100 and Opus at 74/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
